symptoms: Scooter turns off unexpectedly when the steerer is moved or turned
possibleCauses: Display cable is loose, pinched, or damaged

parts.name: Coiled VCU Display Cable(non waterproof connector)

steps.title: Preparation
steps.desc: Power off the scooter. Remove the deck cover and the front mudguard to access internal components.
steps.tools: Hex Key 3mm, Hex Key 2.5mm, Phillips Screwdriver

steps.title: Release Lower Brake Cable
steps.desc: Loosen the pinch bolt at the front wheel using a 3mm hex key. Slide the brake cable out from the pinch bolt cover through the cable guide hole.

steps.title: Release Upper Brake Lever
steps.desc: Pull the brake lever, align the slots in the adjustment nuts, and slide the brake cable out of the lever housing.
steps.tip: Aligning the slots creates an open channel for the inner wire to slide out.

steps.title: Disconnect Display from Handlebar
steps.desc: Remove the handlebar from the top of the steerer tube and unplug the display cable connector.
steps.warning: Pull the connector straight out to avoid bending the pins.

steps.title: Remove Top Cap Grommet
steps.desc: Fold the steerer tube down. Use a flathead screwdriver to pry out the grommet. Use a cutter to slice the grommet in the middle to remove it from the cables.
steps.warning: Work carefully to avoid scratching the paint on the steerer tube.

steps.title: Extract Brake Cable
steps.desc: Pull the brake cable completely out through the top cap hole.

steps.title: Expose Frame Grommet
steps.desc: Remove the two frame grommet cover bolts using a 2.5mm hex screwdriver.

steps.title: Disconnect from Controller
steps.desc: Unplug the display cable from the microcontroller (ESC) inside the deck.

steps.title: Remove Old Cable
steps.desc: Pull the display cable out through the frame grommet hole. Straighten the display cable head and pull it completely out through the frame inlet port and then the top cap hole.

steps.title: Feed New Display Cable
steps.desc: Feed the new cable into the top cap hole, through the frame inlet port, and finally through the frame grommet hole.
steps.tip: If the cable gets stuck in the frame inlet port, gently twist the cable while pushing.

steps.title: Secure Frame Connections
steps.desc: Connect the cable to the microcontroller. Tuck all wires neatly into the frame grommet and reinstall the grommet cover using a 2.5mm hex screwdriver.

steps.title: Reinstall Handlebar
steps.desc: Feed the cable up through the steerer tube, reconnect it to the display, and reinsert the handlebar into the tube.

steps.title: Route & Lock Brake Cable
steps.desc: Feed the brake cable back through the top cap. Reattach it to the brake lever and rotate the adjustment nuts to lock the cable in place.
steps.tip: Ensure the slots on the nuts are no longer aligned after installation.

steps.title: Brake Tensioning
steps.desc: Pull the cable tight at the wheel using pliers while tightening the pinch bolt. Test the lever feel to ensure it is firm.
steps.warning: Do not over-tighten the pinch bolt, as it may damage the cable strands.
steps.tip: Before tightening the pinch bolt firmly, use a small flathead screwdriver to maintain a gap between the fork and pinch bolt cover, then tighten the bolt.
